description_long: This house is situated on a sloped, grassy terrain and features a multi-section hip roof with light tan shingles. The structure is elevated on stilts, particularly at the front, to accommodate the uneven ground, and has a prominent wooden deck or porch area extending from one side. The exterior walls are clad in dark wood, and the house includes large windows, especially on the front and side facades, allowing for ample natural light. A blue-tinted glass or acrylic section, likely a balcony or sunroom, is visible on one side, adding a modern touch to the otherwise rustic design. The house appears spacious, single-storied, and designed to blend with a natural, suburban, or semi-rural environment.
description: A single-story, stilted house with a hip roof, dark wood exterior, large windows, and a blue-tinted balcony, set on a grassy slope.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, elevated porch, and large windows with the house raised on stilts above the sloped terrain.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the blue-tinted balcony or sunroom and the house's integration with the hillside.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the house, the supporting stilts, and the extended deck area.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both the blue balcony and the main structure, emphasizing the house's multi-section roof and stilted foundation.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large windows and a prominent glass section
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a raised porch with steps, wooden railings, and a covered overhang.
